what_it_does: Renders a fixed 1600x900 HTML design showing three ordered rows where position is expressed through colour intensity, labels, rings, and figures.
observations: Imports Plus Jakarta Sans and a shared token stylesheet at /surface/styles/vars.css.
Centers a fixed-size .view on a table-coloured page background.
Uses a radial warm-to-cool background, faint blueprint grid in the top-right, a goldwashed footer band, and a circular V mark.
Content is organized into three labelled rows: stations as pills, hands as circular letter markers, and figures as large numeric stats.
Colour progresses from gold through ochre and bronze toward fainter/darker tones using CSS variables and color-mix().
Inline comments explicitly describe the rows as counted collections stepping one axis in order.
standouts: Ordinal meaning is carried by left-to-right sequence and colour attenuation.
Each row uses a different visual grammar for the same ordered-axis idea: pills, rings, and figures.
The starred 365 figure is visually elevated with larger type and gold colour.
conventions: Uses shared CSS custom properties for colour, spacing, and type scale.
Uses absolute positioning for the main canvas structure and flexbox for row internals.
Uses compact variant class names such as pl1-pl5 and h1-h6 to encode ordered states.
Keeps all page-specific CSS inline in the HTML document.
Uses modern CSS features including color-mix() and mask-image.
concerns: The layout is fixed at 1600x900 with large absolute offsets, so it is not responsive from this file alone.
The visual output depends on external CSS variables and the Google font request resolving successfully.
Several class names are terse and ordinal-specific, which makes their meaning dependent on nearby comments and markup.
Some contrast states intentionally become faint, which may reduce legibility for the later hands.
notes: A static design specimen demonstrating ordinal colour as an axis across three different content rows.
questions: Is the circular V mark a version marker, brand mark, or local notation?
Are the station, hand, and figure labels real data categories or illustrative placeholders?
Should the composition support viewport sizes other than the fixed 1600x900 canvas?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: the file presents position as the carrier of colour through ordered rows.
intention_signals: It appears intended to demonstrate how one ordered axis can be represented consistently across different kinds of UI/content elements without adding icons or extra symbols.
novelty: The file applies the same ordinal-colour principle to three unrelated visual forms in one composed ledger-like specimen.
contribution: Contributes a clear ordinal-colour substrate: ordered collections where sequence, attenuation, and a single highlighted item communicate hierarchy while preserving neutral row structure.
summary_for_embedding: A fixed HTML/CSS design specimen showing three ordered rows of pills, circular markers, and numeric figures where colour intensity indicates position along one axis.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for demonstrating ordinal colour as a readable design principle across multiple content treatments in a token-based visual system.
